title: CBIC Revises Customs Exemption Notifications to Align with Finance Act, 2026
content:
# CBIC Revises Customs Exemption Notifications to Align with Finance Act, 2026

Ek important update hai! Government ne Customs Exemption Notifications ko Finance Act, 2026 ke saath align karne ke liye amendments announce kiye hain. Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) ne is regard mein Notification No. 14/2026-Customs dated April 30, 2026, issue kiya hai.

**Key Highlights:**
*   **Purpose:** The primary objective of this notification is to harmonize and rationalize existing customs exemption notifications with the changes introduced by the Finance Act, 2026.
*   **Scope of Amendments:** It introduces wide-ranging amendments to multiple customs exemption notifications that were issued between 2004 and 2026.
*   **What's Changed:**
    *   Tariff item classifications have been revised.
    *   Certain entries have been substituted, and some serial numbers have been omitted.
    *   Product coverage across various sectors like food products, chemicals, metals, machinery parts, electronics, plastics, and beverages has been updated.
    *   These changes aim to remove inconsistencies, ensure uniform classification, and streamline the customs exemption framework.
*   **Effective Date:** The amendments are effective from May 1, 2026.

This move by CBIC aims to modernize tariff schedules and bring more clarity to duty exemptions, ensuring a smoother customs administration for importers and stakeholders.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.cbic.gov.in/Customs-Notifications)

*Published on: 2 May 2026*
